test.detail: 900 system tests passed + 21 prototype tests = 921 total

lint.command: cargo build --release (Rust compiler warnings only, no separate linter)
lint.detail: Warnings are pre-existing dead_code and unused_variables from test harness imports, not new regressions

nonBlockingIssues.description: MSG_POINTER_ABS sent per-IRQ-batch rather than per-EV_SYN boundary; implicit SHM index coupling (handle 4 assumes SHM index 4); IPC struct definitions duplicated between input driver and compositor

Dirty rect coordinates use raw 'as u16' truncation (safe for current resolutions); dirty rects not clamped to framebuffer bounds before storage (safe due to downstream compositor clamping); potential single-frame ghost cursor during rapid multi-event movement
architecture.md IPC table was missing MSG_SET_CURSOR (type 35) — fixed in this synthesis
~60 lines of Ctrl+Tab context-switch logic duplicated between first and second input channel handlers; only Left Ctrl tracked as modifier (Right Ctrl not handled, pre-existing limitation)

appliedUpdates.description: Added MSG_SET_CURSOR (type 35, compositor → editor) to the Editor ↔ Compositor IPC Protocol table in architecture.md

Documented QEMU reverse device enumeration order in architecture.md Pointer Pipeline section — QEMU enumerates virtio-mmio devices in reverse CLI order, affecting which input channel receives keyboard vs tablet events

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update IPC message type allocation to include types 10-12: '10 = MSG_KEY_EVENT, 11 = MSG_POINTER_ABS, 12 = MSG_POINTER_BUTTON (input driver → compositor)'. Also add type 35 = MSG_SET_CURSOR (compositor → editor).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: AGENTS.md currently lists types 1-7, 8, 20, 30-39, 40+ but omits 10-12 and 35. These were added in the pointer milestone features (virtio-tablet-driver and click-to-position). Future workers allocating new message types may collide with these undocumented ranges.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update test baseline from '869 tests (848 system + 21 prototype)' to '921 tests (900 system + 21 prototype)'. The count has grown across the pointer milestone features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Test run shows 900 system tests passing. AGENTS.md says 869 (848+21). Stale baselines confuse workers about whether they broke or added tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: When handling events from multiple input channels, extract shared event-processing logic into a helper function rather than duplicating between channel handlers. This prevents maintenance risk from lockstep update requirements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: full-demo-validation feature: ~60 lines of identical Ctrl+Tab logic copy-pasted between two input channel handlers in compositor/main.rs. Worker acknowledged this as technical debt. The visual-overhaul-cleanup feature already includes extracting this duplication.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: os-worker skill should document: (1) xy_to_byte() exists in the drawing library for click-to-text-position conversion, (2) QEMU reverse device enumeration order affects which input channel receives keyboard events when virtio-tablet-device is present, (3) for pointer visual verification, temporary CURSOR_VISIBLE=true hack forces cursor rendering since QEMU doesn't route mouse events in headless mode.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three separate workers surfaced these as skill gaps: click-to-position worker suggested noting xy_to_byte, full-demo-validation worker spent ~20 tool calls debugging device ordering, cursor-surface worker used CURSOR_VISIBLE hack and suggested documenting it.

rejectedObservations.observation: cursor-surface: AGENTS.md already documents QEMU headless mouse limitation
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — AGENTS.md Visual Verification section explicitly covers this for milestone 3

rejectedObservations.observation: cursor-surface: qemu-mouse service config already in services.yaml
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — services.yaml already has qemu-mouse service with -device virtio-tablet-device
